title: jasminum fluminense subsp. gratissimum
intro: Jasminum fluminense subsp. gratissimum, also known as the Night-blooming Jasmine, is a plant species from the family Oleaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America and Central America. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Jasminum fluminense subsp. gratissimum include its antispasmodic, sedative, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the mind and body. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or essential oils, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
